title: Metal Fleet
description: Live state + health of the bare-metal Firecracker fleet (apps/metal-agent hosts routed by apps/api metal-warm-pool-controller). Router counters/histogram (metal.assignments, metal.cold_miss, metal.host_errors, metal.no_host, metal.wake_latency_ms) come from the API; per-host gauges (metal.host.*) are folded from each host's heartbeat. OTel dots become underscores in PromQL. Heartbeats round-robin across API replicas, so per-host gauges use `max by (host_id)` to dedupe. Imported via SigNoz UI -> Dashboards -> Import JSON, or POST /api/v1/dashboards.

widgets.title: Live hosts (by region)
widgets.description: metal_host_up summed per region. Compare against desired baseline in config/metal-fleet.ts (drift = missing/dead host).

widgets.title: Snapshot hit-rate (5m)
widgets.description: Fraction of resolutions served by a warm/cold resume vs a fresh cold claim. The headline sleep/wake effectiveness number.

widgets.title: No-host fallbacks (5m)
widgets.description: getMetalProjectUrl found no live host (fell back to Knative, or 503 in metal-only). Should be 0.

widgets.title: FC processes vs tracked VMs (leak guard)
widgets.description: metal_host_fc_procs should equal available+assigned. A persistent gap is the churn process-leak fingerprint (see MetalFcProcessLeak alert).

widgets.title: Utilization % (assigned/poolSize) — burst trigger basis
widgets.description: Burst policy scales up above 70%, down below 40% (config/metal-fleet.ts).

widgets.title: NVMe used % (by host)
widgets.description: GC high-watermark shedding kicks in near the top; sustained >85% -> MetalHostDiskPressure alert.

widgets.title: Assignments by source (local / store / none)
widgets.description: local = sub-second NVMe resume; store = S3 pull; none = fresh cold claim. A shift toward store/none means the local cache is cold.

widgets.title: Why VMs are live (fleet): app-users / agent-turns / idle-tail
widgets.description: Decomposes assigned (running) VMs by liveness class — app_active (serving /api/* end-user traffic) + agent_active (an agent chat turn in flight) + idle_tail (running but neither). The three sum to metal_host_assigned; a large idle_tail is the open-tab/30-min-suspend-window churn. Series only appear for hosts/guests on the new build, so a lagging total during rollout is expected.

widgets.title: App-users vs agent-turns (by host)
widgets.description: Per-host app_active (real end-user app traffic) and agent_active (agent chat turns in flight). Lets you spot a host carrying a live published app vs one full of editor sessions.
